title: Revenue requirement for name change within one year
text: if it has changed its name within the last one year, at least fifty per cent. of the revenue, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, for the preceding one full year has been earned by it from the activity indicated by its new name.
notes: Map to issuer_name_change and revenue requirement (50% from new activity).

source.span_hint: if it has changed its name within

title: Eligibility for IPO through book-building process
text: An issuer not satisfying the condition stipulated in sub-regulation (1) shall be eligible to make an initial public offer only if the issue is made through the book-building process and the issuer undertakes to allot at least seventy five per cent. of the net offer to qualified institutional buyers and to refund the full subscription money if it fails to do so.

source.span_hint: An issuer not satisfying the condition

title: Technology-intensive use requirement for SR equity shares issuance
text: the issuer shall be intensive in the use of technology, information technology, intellectual property, data analytics, bio-technology or nano-technology to provide products, services or business platforms with substantial value addition.

source.span_hint: the issuer shall be intensive

title: Net worth of SR shareholder requirement
text: the net worth of the SR shareholder, as determined by a Registered Valuer, shall not be more than rupees one thousand crore.

source.span_hint: the net worth of the SR shareholder

title: SR equity shares issuance authorization requirement
text: The issue of SR equity shares had been authorized by a special resolution passed at a general meeting of the shareholders of the issuer, where the notice calling for such general meeting specifically provided for - the size of issue of SR equity shares, ratio of voting rights of SR equity shares vis-à-vis the ordinary shares.

source.span_hint: The issue of SR equity shares

title: SR equity shares holding period requirement
text: the SR equity shares have been issued prior to the filing of draft red herring prospectus and held for a period of at least three months prior to the filing of the red herring prospectus.

source.span_hint: the SR equity shares have been issued

title: SR equity shares voting rights ratio requirement
text: The SR equity shares shall have voting rights in the ratio of a minimum of 2:1 upto a maximum of 10:1 compared to ordinary shares and such ratio shall be in whole numbers only.

source.span_hint: The SR equity shares shall have voting rights

title: Application for in-principle approval to stock exchanges
text: it has made an application to one or more stock exchanges to seek an in-principle approval for listing of its specified securities on such stock exchanges and has chosen one of them as the designated stock exchange, in terms of Schedule XIX.

source.span_hint: it has made an application

title: Agreement with depository for dematerialisation
text: it has entered into an agreement with a depository for dematerialisation of the specified securities already issued and proposed to be issued.

source.span_hint: it has entered into an agreement

title: Promoters' securities in dematerialised form
text: all its specified securities held by the promoters are in dematerialised form prior to filing of the offer document.

source.span_hint: all its specified securities held

title: Partly paid-up equity shares requirement
text: all its existing partly paid-up equity shares have either been fully paid-up or have been forfeited.

source.span_hint: all its existing partly paid-up

title: Limit on amount raised for general corporate purposes and unspecified acquisitions/investments
text: (i) general corporate purposes, and (ii) such objects where the issuer company has not identified acquisition or investment target, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document, shall not exceed thirty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer: Provided that the amount raised for such objects where the issuer company has not identified acquisition or investment target, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document, shall not exceed twenty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer:
notes: Limits on funds allocation for general corporate purposes and unspecified acquisitions/investments.

source.span_hint: general corporate purposes

title: Holding period for equity shares offered for sale
text: Only such fully paid-up equity shares may be offered for sale to the public, which have been held by the sellers for a period of at least one year prior to the filing of the draft offer document: Provided that in case the equity shares received on conversion or exchange of fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities including depository receipts are being offered for sale, the holding period of such convertible securities, including depository receipts, as well as that of resultant equity shares together shall be considered for the purpose of calculation of one year period referred in this sub-regulation.
notes: Minimum holding period requirement for equity shares offered for sale.

source.span_hint: Only such fully paid-up

title: Exceptions to holding period requirement for equity shares offered for sale
text: (a) in case of an offer for sale of a government company or statutory authority or corporation or any special purpose vehicle set up and controlled by any one or more of them, which is engaged in the infrastructure sector; (b) if the equity shares offered for sale were acquired pursuant to any scheme approved by a High Court 34[***] or approved by a tribunal or the Central Government under the sections 230 to 234 of Companies Act, 2013, as applicable, in lieu of business and invested capital which had been in existence for a period of more than one year prior to approval of such scheme; (c) if the equity shares offered for sale were issued under a bonus issue on securities held for a period of at least one year prior to the filing of the draft offer document with the Board and further subject to the following: (i) such specified securities being issued out of free reserves and share premium existing in the books of account as at the end of the financial year preceding the financial year in which the draft offer document is filed with the Board; and (ii) such equity shares not being issued by utilisation of revaluation reserves or unrealized profits of the issuer.
notes: Exceptions to holding period requirement for specific cases.

source.span_hint: in case of an offer

title: Limit on shares offered for sale by major shareholders
text: (a) shares offered for sale to the public by shareholder(s) holding, individually or with persons acting in concert, more than twenty per cent of pre-issue shareholding of the issuer based on fully diluted basis, shall not exceed more than fifty per cent of their pre-issue shareholding on fully diluted basis;
notes: Limit on shares offered for sale by major shareholders.

source.span_hint: shares offered for sale

title: Limit on shares offered for sale by minor shareholders
text: (b) shares offered for sale to the public by shareholder(s) holding, individually or with persons acting in concert, less than twenty per cent of pre-issue shareholding of the issuer based on fully diluted basis, shall not exceed more than ten per cent of pre-issue shareholding of the issuer on fully diluted basis;
notes: Limit on shares offered for sale by minor shareholders.

source.span_hint: less than twenty per cent

title: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares
text: The issuer shall not convert its optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares unless the holders of such convertible debt instruments have sent their positive consent to the issuer and non-receipt of reply to any notice sent by the issuer for this purpose shall not be construed as consent for conversion of any convertible debt instruments.

source.reg: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares
source.span_hint: The issuer shall not convert

title: Option for holders to not convert convertible debt instruments into equity shares
text: Where the value of the convertible portion of any listed convertible debt instruments issued by an issuer exceeds ten crore rupees and the issuer has not determined the conversion price of such convertible debt instruments at the time of making the issue, the holders of such convertible debt instruments shall be given the option of not converting the convertible portion into equity shares.

source.reg: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares (sub-regulation 11(2))
source.span_hint: Where the value of the convertible portion

title: Redemption of unconverted optionally convertible debt instruments
text: Where an option is to be given to the holders of the convertible debt instruments in terms of sub-regulation (2) and if one or more of such holders do not exercise the option to convert the instruments into equity share capital at a price determined in the general meeting of the shareholders, the issuer shall redeem that part of the instruments within one month from the last date by which option is to be exercised, at a price which shall not be less than its face value.

source.reg: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares (sub-regulation 11(3))
source.span_hint: Where an option is to be given

title: Issue of convertible debt instruments for financing or providing loans/acquiring shares
text: An issuer shall not issue convertible debt instruments for financing or for providing loans to or for acquiring shares of any person who is part of the promoter group or group companies: Provided that an issuer shall be eligible to issue fully convertible debt instruments for these purposes if the period of conversion of such debt instruments is less than eighteen months from the date of issue of such debt instruments.

source.reg: Issue of convertible debt instruments for financing or providing loans/acquiring shares
source.span_hint: An issuer shall not issue

title: Issue of warrants in an initial public offer
text: An issuer shall be eligible to issue warrants in an initial public offer subject to the following: a) the tenure of such warrants shall not exceed eighteen months from the date of their allotment in the initial public offer; b) a specified security may have one or more warrants attached to it; c) the price or formula for determination of exercise price of the warrants shall be determined upfront and disclosed in the offer document and at least twenty-five per cent. of the consideration amount based on the exercise price shall also be received upfront;

source.reg: Issue of warrants in an initial public offer
source.span_hint: An issuer shall be eligible

title: Consideration amount for warrants based on exercise price
text: the price or formula for determination of exercise price of the warrants shall be determined upfront and disclosed in the offer document and at least twenty-five per cent. of the consideration amount based on the exercise price shall also be received upfront;

source.reg: Issue of warrants in an initial public offer (sub-regulation 13(c))
source.span_hint: the price or formula for determination

title: Minimum promoters’ contribution in post-issue capital
text: The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least twenty per cent. of the post-issue capital: Provided that in case the post-issue shareholding of the promoters is less than twenty per cent., alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India 37[or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s)] may contribute to meet the shortfall in minimum contribution as specified for the promoters, subject to a maximum of ten per cent. of the post-issue capital without being identified as promoter(s). Provided further that the requirement of minimum promoters’ contribution shall not apply in case an issuer does not have any identifiable promoter.

source.reg: Minimum promoters’ contribution in post-issue capital
source.span_hint: The promoters of the issuer

title: Tenure of Warrants ≤ 18 months
text: the tenure of such warrants shall not exceed eighteen months from the date of their allotment in the initial public offer;

source.span_hint: tenure of such warrants shall not exceed

title: Multiple Warrants per Security Allowed
text: a specified security may have one or more warrants attached to it;

source.span_hint: a specified security may have one or more

title: Cap Price Consideration in Formula-Based Exercise Price
text: Provided that in case the exercise price of warrants is based on a formula, twenty-five per cent. consideration amount based on the cap price of the price band determined for the linked equity shares or convertible securities shall be received upfront.

source.span_hint: exercise price of warrants is based on a formula

title: Forfeiture for Non-Exercise Within 3 Months
text: in case the warrant holder does not exercise the option to take equity shares against any of the warrants held by the warrant holder, within three months from the date of payment of consideration, such consideration made in respect of such warrants shall be forfeited by the issuer.

source.span_hint: warrant holder does not exercise the option

title: Minimum Promoters’ Contribution ≥ 20%
text: The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least twenty per cent. of the post-issue capital:

source.span_hint: promoters of the issuer shall hold at least twenty

title: Alternative Contribution for Shortfall
text: Provided that in case the post-issue shareholding of the promoters is less than twenty per cent., alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India 37[or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s)] may contribute to meet the shortfall in minimum contribution as specified for the promoters, subject to a maximum of ten per cent. of the post-issue capital without being identified as promoter(s).

source.span_hint: post-issue shareholding of the promoters is less than twenty

title: No Requirement if No Promoter
text: Provided further that the requirement of minimum promoters’ contribution shall not apply in case an issuer does not have any identifiable promoter.

source.span_hint: requirement of minimum promoters’ contribution shall not apply

title: Promoters Contribution by Equity or Convertible Securities
text: the promoters shall contribute twenty per cent. as stipulated in sub-regulation (1), as the case may be, either by way of equity shares38[, including SR equity shares held, if any,] or by way of subscription to convertible securities:

source.span_hint: the promoters shall contribute twenty per cent.

title: Pre-Determined Conversion Price Requirement
text: Provided that if the price of the equity shares allotted pursuant to conversion is not pre-determined and not disclosed in the offer document, the promoters shall contribute only by way of subscription to the convertible securities being issued in the public issue and shall undertake in writing to subscribe to the equity shares pursuant to conversion of such securities.

source.span_hint: price of the equity shares allotted pursuant to conversion

title: Weighted Average Price for Different Conversion Dates
text: in case of any issue of convertible securities which are convertible or exchangeable on different dates and if the promoters’ contribution is by way of equity shares (conversion price being pre-determined), such contribution shall not be at a price lower than the weighted average price of the equity share capital arising out of conversion of such securities.

source.span_hint: convertible or exchangeable on different dates

title: Promoters Contribution for Convertible Debt Instruments
text: subject to the provisions of clause (a) and (b) above, in case of an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments without a prior public issue of equity shares, the promoters shall bring in a contribution of at least twenty per cent. of the project cost in the form of equity shares, subject to contributing at least twenty per cent. of the issue size from their own funds in the form of equity shares:

source.span_hint: initial public offer of convertible debt instruments

title: Staged Project Contribution Requirement
text: Provided that if the project is to be implemented in stages, the promoters’ contribution shall be with respect to total equity participation till the respective stage vis-à-vis the debt raised or proposed to be raised through the public issue.

source.span_hint: project is to be implemented in stages

title: Satisfaction of Requirements One Day Prior
text: The promoters shall satisfy the requirements of this regulation at least one day prior to the date of opening of the issue.

source.span_hint: requirements of this regulation at least one day

title: Escrow Account for Promoters Contribution
text: In case the promoters have to subscribe to equity shares or convertible securities towards minimum promoters’ contribution, the amount of promoters’ contribution shall be kept in an escrow account with a scheduled commercial bank, which shall be released to the issuer along with the release of the issue proceeds:

source.span_hint: subscribe to equity shares or convertible securities

title: Disclosure of Use of Funds in Offer Document
text: Provided that where the promoters’ contribution has already been brought in and utilised, the issuer shall give the cash flow statement disclosing the use of such funds in the offer document;

source.span_hint: promoters’ contribution has already been brought in

title: Partial Contribution for Large Amounts
text: Provided further that where the minimum promoters’ contribution is more than one hundred crore rupees and the initial public offer is for partly paid shares, the promoters shall bring in at least one hundred crore rupees before the date of opening of the issue and the remaining amount may be brought on a pro-rata basis before the calls are made to the public.

source.span_hint: minimum promoters’ contribution is more than one hundred crore

title: Minimum promoters' contribution lock-in period
text: The specified securities held by the promoters shall not be transferable (hereinafter referred to as “lock-in”) for a period of eighteen months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer, provided that if the majority of the issue proceeds excluding the portion of offer for sale is proposed to be utilized for capital expenditure, then the lock-in period shall be three years.
notes: Minimum promoters' contribution lock-in period. If used for capital expenditure, extended to 3 years.

source.span_hint: minimum promoters’ contribution including contribution made

title: Promoters' holding in excess of minimum lock-in period
text: promoters’ holding in excess of minimum promoters’ contribution shall be locked-in for a period of six months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer, provided that if the majority of the issue proceeds excluding the portion of offer for sale is proposed to be utilized for capital expenditure, then the lock-in period shall be one year.
notes: Promoters' holding over minimum contribution lock-in period. If used for capital expenditure, extended to 1 year.

source.span_hint: promoters’ holding in excess of minimum

title: Employee stock options lock-in period
text: equity shares allotted to employees, whether currently an employee or not, under an employee stock option or employee stock purchase scheme of the issuer prior to the initial public offer, if the issuer has made full disclosures with respect to such options or scheme in accordance with Part A of Schedule VI.
notes: Employee stock options lock-in period. Exempted from general lock-in rules if disclosed properly.

source.span_hint: equity shares allotted to employees

title: Employee stock option trust lock-in period
text: equity shares held by an employee stock option trust or transferred to the employees by an employee stock option trust pursuant to exercise of options by the employees, whether currently employees or not, in accordance with the employee stock option plan or employee stock purchase scheme.
notes: Employee stock option trust lock-in period. Exempted from general lock-in rules if disclosed properly.

source.span_hint: equity shares held by an employee

title: Venture capital fund lock-in period
text: equity shares held by a venture capital fund or alternative investment fund of category I or Category II or a foreign venture capital investor, provided that such equity shares shall be locked in for a period of at least six months from the date of purchase.
notes: Venture capital fund lock-in period. Minimum 6 months lock-in period.

source.span_hint: equity shares held by a venture

title: Exclusion for stabilising agent under green shoe option
text: The lock-in provisions shall not apply with respect to the specified securities lent to stabilising agent for the purpose of green shoe option, during the period starting from the date of.

source.span_hint: lock-in provisions shall not apply

title: Holding period for converted equity shares
text: For the purpose of clause (c), in case such equity shares have resulted pursuant to conversion of fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities, the holding period of such convertible securities as well as that of resultant equity shares together shall be considered for the purpose of calculation of six months period and convertible securities shall be deemed to be fully paid-up, if the entire consideration payable thereon has been paid and no further consideration is payable at the time of their conversion.
notes: Map to holding_period for converted equity shares and convertible securities.

source.span_hint: conversion of fully paid-up compulsorily

title: Bonus issue equity shares holding period
text: (a) that the bonus shares being issued out of free reserves and share premium existing in the books of account as at the end of the financial year preceding the financial year in which the draft offer document is filed with the Board;
notes: Map to holding_period for bonus issue equity shares.

source.span_hint: bonus shares being issued out of free

title: Bonus issue equity shares restrictions
text: (b) that the bonus shares not being issued by utilisation of revaluation reserves or unrealized profits of the issuer.
notes: Map to restrictions on issuing bonus shares from specific reserves.

source.span_hint: not being issued by utilisation of revaluation

title: Equity shares include bonus issue equity shares
text: For the purpose of clauses (a) and (b), equity shares shall include any equity shares allotted pursuant to a bonus issue against equity shares allotted pursuant to an employee stock option or employee stock purchase scheme or a stock appreciation right scheme.
notes: Map to inclusion of specific types of equity shares in calculation.

source.span_hint: equity shares allotted pursuant

title: Lock-in for specified securities lent to stabilising agent
text: The lock-in provisions shall not apply with respect to the specified securities lent to stabilising agent for the purpose of green shoe option, during the period starting from the date of lending of such specified securities and ending on the date on which they are returned to the lender in terms of sub-regulation (5) or (6) of regulation 57: Provided that the specified securities shall be locked-in for the remaining period from the date on which they are returned to the lender.
notes: Map to lock_in_period for lent securities.

source.span_hint: lock-in provisions shall not apply

title: Lock-in for partly paid-up securities
text: If the specified securities which are subject to lock-in are partly paid-up and the amount called-up on such specified securities is less than the amount called-up on the specified securities issued to the public, the lock-in shall end only on the expiry of three years after such specified securities have become pari passu with the specified securities issued to the public.
notes: Map to lock_in_period for partly paid-up securities.

source.span_hint: partly paid-up and the amount called-up

title: Inscription or recording of non-transferability
text: The certificates of specified securities which are subject to lock-in shall contain the inscription “non-transferable” and specify the lock-in period and in case such specified securities are dematerialised, the issuer shall ensure that the lock-in is recorded by the depository.
notes: Map to inscription or recording of non-transferability on certificates.

source.span_hint: certificates of specified securities

title: Pledge of locked-in specified securities by promoters
text: (a) if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (a) of regulation 16, the loan has been granted to the issuer company or its subsidiary(ies) for the purpose of financing one or more of the objects of the issue and pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan;
notes: Map to conditions for pledging locked-in securities by promoters.

source.span_hint: pledge of specified securities

title: Pledge of locked-in specified securities by others
text: (b) if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (b) of regulation 16 and the pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan.
notes: Map to conditions for pledging locked-in securities by others.

source.span_hint: pledge of specified securities

title: Pledge of locked-in specified securities by promoters
text: Specified securities held by the promoters and locked-in may be pledged as a collateral security for a loan granted by a scheduled commercial bank or a public financial institution or a systemically important non-banking finance company or a housing finance company, subject to the following:
notes: Pledge conditions for promoters' locked-in securities.

source.span_hint: Specified securities held by the promoters

title: Conditions for pledge under clause (a) of regulation 16
text: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (a) of regulation 16, the loan has been granted to the issuer company or its subsidiary(ies) for the purpose of financing one or more of the objects of the issue and pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan;
notes: Conditions for pledge under clause (a) of regulation 16.

source.span_hint: clause (a) of regulation 16

title: Conditions for pledge under clause (b) of regulation 16
text: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (b) of regulation 16 and the pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan.
notes: Conditions for pledge under clause (b) of regulation 16.

source.span_hint: clause (b) of regulation 16

title: Transferability of locked-in specified securities
text: Subject to the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition of shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011, the specified securities held by the promoters and locked-in as per regulation 16, may be transferred to another promoter or any person of the promoter group or a new promoter and the specified securities held by persons other than the promoters and locked-in as per regulation 17, may be transferred to any other person holding the specified securities which are locked-in along with the securities proposed to be transferred:

source.span_hint: Subject to the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board

title: Appointment of lead managers, other intermediaries, and compliance officer
text: The issuer shall appoint one or more merchant bankers, which are registered with the Board, as lead manager(s) to the issue.

source.span_hint: The issuer shall appoint one or more merchant bankers

title: Rights, obligations, and responsibilities of lead managers
text: Where the issue is managed by more than one lead manager, the rights, obligations and responsibilities, relating inter alia to disclosures, allotment, refund and underwriting obligations, if any, of each lead manager shall be predetermined and be disclosed in the draft offer document and the offer document as specified in Schedule I.
notes: Rights, obligations, and responsibilities for multiple lead managers.

source.span_hint: Where the issue is managed by more than one

title: Non-associate lead manager requirement
text: At least one lead manager to the issue shall not be an associate (as defined under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Merchant Bankers) Regulations, 1992) of the issuer and if any of the lead manager is an associate of the issuer, it shall disclose itself as an associate of the issuer and its role shall be limited to marketing of the issue.
notes: Non-associate requirement for at least one lead manager.

source.span_hint: At least one lead manager to the issue

title: Appointment of other intermediaries
text: The issuer shall, in consultation with the lead manager(s), appoint other intermediaries which are registered with the Board after the lead manager(s) have independently assessed the capability of other intermediaries to carry out their obligations.

source.span_hint: The issuer shall, in consultation with the lead manager(s)

title: Agreements with intermediaries
text: The issuer shall enter into an agreement with the lead manager(s) in the format specified in Schedule II and enter into agreements with other intermediaries as required under the respective regulations applicable to the intermediary concerned:
notes: Agreement requirements for lead managers and other intermediaries.

source.span_hint: The issuer shall enter into an agreement with the
